description:
Winter Operating Area: Madagascar, Maldives, Seychelles
Summer Operating Area: Maldives, Seychelles

MARIE JOSEPH is a 23.99m / 79ft Catamaran Luxury Yacht built in 2021. Sleeping 8 guests in 4 cabins and with a crew of 4. Cruising at 8.00 kn/ph with a top speed of 11.00 kn/ph. The guest cabins comprise of a Master cabin, a VIP cabin and two twin cabins, all with en-suite bathroom facilities.

blueprint.name: MARIE JOSEPH
tenders: 1 x Williams SportJet 520 Tender | 7 passenger capacity

crew.name: Denis Schuldt
crew.bio:
Nationality: German
Languages: German, Turkish, English

Bio:
Captain Denis brings a lifetime of experience at sea, with a career shaped by both private and charter operations across the Mediterranean. Having grown up sailing from a young age, Denis developed his maritime foundation through early dinghy sailing before progressing into professional yachting. 

With extensive experience commanding Sunreef catamarans, Denis has captained vessels ranging from 60ft to 80ft, operating throughout Greece, Turkey, France, and Italy. His background includes full responsibility for yacht operations, navigation, itinerary planning, crew management, safety, and technical oversight, as well as handling chase boats and tenders. 

Denis combines strong technical knowledge with a calm, solutions-focused approach on board, supported by earlier experience as a marine technician working on refits, engine maintenance, and general yacht upkeep. He is known for his professionalism, hands-on leadership style, and ability to deliver smooth, well-organised cruising experiences. 

Certified to Yachtmaster 499 GT with ongoing advancement, Captain Denis offers guests and owners a confident, reliable presence on board, ensuring both safe operations and an enjoyable time at sea.

crew.name: Sitraka Adrianjafimanana
crew.bio:
Nationality: Madagascar
Languages: French, English

Bio: 
Sitraka is an experienced chef with a strong background in luxury hospitality and yachting.
Over the past decade, he has held key positions in high-pressure environments, including
Executive Chef at the Louvre Hotel **** in Madagascar, where he led a team of 21 kitchen
staff, developed innovative training programs, and introduced sustainable menu options.
Transitioning to the yachting industry, Sitraka worked as Chef aboard the Sunreef Yacht 80, 
ATHENA TOO, where he was responsible for creating seasonal menus, managing galley
operations, and catering to a variety of dietary preferences, including meals for children and
crew. As well as cooking, he is also passionate for jazz, and outdoor activities such as football
and cycling.

In the Summer he did a  successful charter season on Seazen II , a Sunreef 70.

As the chef, Sitraka is responsible for the crew and guest meals throughout the charter, ensuring that the food is enjoyable, the interior of the yacht is clean and serviceable, and that all dietary requirements are met. He is a creative cook, varying her meals to provide variety and according to the location of SEAZEN II, to have as much relevance as possible to the region being visited. 

Qualifications and Certificates 
• STCW 
• HACCP Certificate 
• CAP Food Production 
• Diploma in Culinary Arts, Havila School Madagascar

crew.name: Joshua Raine Massey
crew.bio:
Joshua is a motivated and hardworking deckhand with solid experience on large motor yachts ranging from 48m to 95m, including transatlantic crossings, charter preparation, and intensive guest operations. With a strong foundation in marine engineering and hands-on deck work, he is confident in watchkeeping, tender operations, mooring and anchoring, bunkering, crane operations for toys, and all aspects of exterior maintenance.

He has worked in demanding environments with high guest numbers, where safety, attention to detail, and professionalism are essential. Known for thriving under pressure, he brings a positive attitude, strong teamwork skills, and a genuine eagerness to learn and improve. His background in passenger vessels and coaching further reinforces his communication skills, reliability, and ability to support both crew and guests effectively.

crew.name: Melissa Power
crew.bio:
Nationality: British
Languages: English

Melissa is a motivated and hardworking stewardess with experience onboard charter yachts, where she has developed a strong foundation in interior operations and guest service. She completed a full seasonal contract onboard M/Y Lord of the Highlands, a 47.85m charter yacht, supporting the interior team during a busy seven-month season.

Her primary responsibilities included housekeeping, table styling, flower arranging, and regular guest interaction, with a strong focus on maintaining high standards and creating a welcoming onboard environment. Melissa is known for her attention to detail, positive attitude, and ability to adapt quickly in fast-paced charter settings. 

Trained in STCW Basic Safety, Food Safety, Powerboat Level 2, and Crisis & Crowd Management, Melissa brings a professional, eager-to-learn approach to every role. Friendly, reliable, and highly motivated, she is keen to continue developing her career as a stewardess within the yachting industry.
